items.title: Why TV renovation shows are a 'huge gamble' for their creators
items.summary: A planned 12-month build that's still underway three years later is the running joke of design shows. But what happens when you're a TV producer trying to make a series of it?

items.title: UN envoy warns Woodside's North West Shelf extension could violate human rights
items.summary: An independent expert has urged Australia to "make sure that things are done properly", while Woodside pushed back against her involvement.

items.title: Matteo Berrettini withdraws injured at French Open as Matteo Arnaldi progresses to face Flavio Cobolli
items.summary: Matteo Berrettini is once again forced out of a match by injury, retiring in the second set of his French Open quarterfinal with compatriot Matteo Arnaldi.

items.title: RBA governor says 'practically no-one is in negative equity' — as it happened
items.summary: RBA Governor Michele Bullock has told Senate estimates that "practically no-one is in negative equity". Meanwhile, the ASX remains in the red.

items.title: Alleged intruder shot after stabbing couple during home invasion near Tenterfield
items.summary: Two people in their 70s have been critically injured and an alleged intruder has been shot during an alleged home invasion in northern NSW this morning.

items.title: Second Chance Choir helping people overcome hardship through music
items.summary: In an unassuming building in Perth's north, the Second Chance Choir is bringing people together through music to help them put aside years of trauma, violence and substance abuse.

items.title: Keir Starmer urges calm after protests over handcuffed Henry Nowak's death
items.summary: Video of officers ignoring the pleas of a dying, innocent man has sparked a political storm about how police treat ‌different ethnicities.

items.title: Mental health specialists from war-torn Ukraine turn to NSW town for trauma response strategies
items.summary: Specialists from war-torn Ukraine are heading to regional New South Wales for trauma response and rehabilitation training.

items.title: Kakadu's Jim Jim and Twin Falls to stay closed throughout NT's peak tourism season
items.summary: Parks Australia has announced popular Kakadu attractions Jim Jim Falls and Twin Falls will remain closed for the rest of the year, with one tourism operator saying the closures will lead to a "very hard" tourism season.

items.title: Aryna Sabalenka 'just wants to quit tennis' after French Open quarterfinal loss to Diana Shnaider
items.summary: Aryna Sabalenka feels the pain of a shock loss in the quarterfinals of the French Open, saying she just feels like smashing things.

items.title: Leanne Castley is already partyless after quitting the Canberra Liberals but some want her gone entirely
items.summary: Whether they're being driven away or choosing to jump in a getaway car, the women who have recently led the Canberra Liberals are no longer anywhere near the driver's seat.

items.title: First Nations voters sent to back of queue for Voice election
items.summary: South Australia's Electoral Commission was aware that forcing First Nations voters to queue twice for the Voice election would be perceived as "discriminatory behaviour". Despite that, voters say it still happened and, in some cases, they were turned away.

items.title: Extreme heatwaves have become a major threat to Australia's food supply, and not just in summer
items.summary: Record-breaking heatwaves decimated crops in Australia this year and served as a "real wake-up call" for the urgent need to develop more heat-resistant crops and research into food security.

items.title: Child sex offender Cameron Bloomfield given disability award after admin error
items.summary: Repeat child sex offender Cameron Bloomfield was crowned as Victoria's "disability pride champion" in 2024, after a government department admin error.

items.title: As Victor Wembanyama makes his NBA Finals debut, the league's new face is on its biggest stage
items.summary: After years of searching, the NBA finally has its new face in Victor Wembanyama, and he is about to grace the league's biggest stage.

items.title: Puppy farm anger sees breeder withdraw proposal for facility with mandatory caesareans
items.summary: An application for a proposed puppy farm in central Victoria has been withdrawn after lobbying by nearby residents and animal activists.

items.title: Australia chasing men's rugby sevens World Championship at final tournament in Bordeaux
items.summary: An unbeaten tournament victory in Spain gives Australia a chance of claiming the men's World Championship, with the title to be decided in this weekend's final round.

items.title: Queensland beekeepers paying thousands each month to fend off varroa mites as colonies devastated
items.summary: Beekeepers in Queensland say they are "barely hanging on" a year after the varroa mite was detected in the state for the first time.

items.title: Wagga Wagga's homeless say they have 'nowhere to go' despite emergency response
items.summary: As governments work to meet the growing need for social housing, community groups are stepping up to meet the rapidly increasing demand for their services to help fill the gap.

items.title: NSW government to introduce medicinal cannabis driving reforms

items.title: Tasmania reconsiders bridge ban as Nuyina's refuelling saga drags on
items.summary: Authorities are considering whether it is feasible for Australia's Antarctic icebreaker to safely travel under Hobart's Tasman Bridge, as the Tasmanian government tries to resolve a costly refuelling saga.

items.title: Australia's economic slowdown is just beginning, economists warn, as recession risks rise
items.summary: The economy grew just 0.3 per cent in the first quarter, gross domestic product per capita went backwards, and interest rate rises and cost-of-living pressures are weighing on households.

items.title: Who could replace Hobart's outgoing lord mayor Anna Reynolds?
items.summary: After eight years in the top role, Anna Reynolds has announced she will not seek re-election in October. Her departure coincides with a notable reduction in the number of council representatives.

items.title: What do the pope and most Australians have in common? Neither trust AI companies
items.summary: Most Australians appear to distrust and dislike artificial intelligence despite using technology more than people in other countries.

items.summary: Fresh access to AI deemed too dangerous for public release has ramped up pressure on the government to respond to possible risks.

items.title: Sportsbet owner ventures into giveaways with rewards club, anti-gambling advocates concerned

items.title: Falling house prices bring relief for some while a financial nightmare for others

items.title: Rubio says concerns over Trump's health 'absurd and ridiculous'
items.summary: The US president's mental acuity is in the spotlight, with the secretary of state shown videos of Donald Trump appearing to fall asleep during public appearances.

items.title: AI may be tightening jobs market, but some roles could see stronger demand

items.title: Former head of Indonesian free school meals program arrested by attorney-general

items.summary: Queensland childcare worker Edwina Ling has been jailed for four years after pleading guilty to more than 80 counts of assault involving three young children.

items.title: Man dies in custody after crashing ute into Mudgee police station

items.title: Alice Springs's Tangentyere CEO Walter Shaw defends state of town camp housing
items.summary: After the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, the state of Alice Springs's town camps shocked the nation. Now, the man in charge of servicing them is speaking out.

items.title: Mother granted bail after allegedly trying to print child abuse material at Canberra store
items.summary: The woman's defence argues her daughter took and tried to print the photographs herself for a creative project and they were not predatory in nature.

items.summary: Former construction union boss John Setka has faced court fighting allegations he sent abusive emails, with his lawyer saying he has received threats on his life.

items.summary: After three days of searching, South Australian police are no closer to finding out what happened to missing mother of two Trisha Graf.

items.summary: A south-west Victorian football club has apologised after it allowed a convicted sex offender to return to the club after a stint in jail.

items.title: Sydney Morning Herald takes down opinion piece about AI after author used AI to write it
items.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald has taken down an opinion piece defending the use of artificial intelligence at universities, after it emerged its author used AI to write it.

items.summary: Private equity baron Joel Thickins is alleged to have crashed his BMW into five cars in Sydney's east, before refusing two alcohol-detecting breath tests.

items.title: Can one optimal pathway have two lanes? When it comes to AUKUS submarines, apparently yes
items.summary: Scrutiny of AUKUS's delivery reveals two "optimal pathways" for Australia's submarines.

items.title: ABS estimates 80pc of tobacco consumed in Australia last year illegal amid 'rapid growth' in black market
items.summary: The experimental estimates are based on "nicotine metabolite concentrations" detected in wastewater samples, combined with data showing a collapse in spending on legal cigarettes and vapes.

items.title: Gold reserves at Kalgoorlie Super Pit continue to grow with millions spent on underground drilling

items.summary: Ali Brigginshaw relinquishes the Broncos captaincy in her final NRLW season and reveals her view about a club meeting room being renamed in her honour.

items.title: Water Corp defends response to leaks and sewage spills amid flood of complaints

items.title: Brisbane man charged under new state laws after allegedly plotting violent attack on family member
items.summary: A court heard Atallah Mohaghegh Nahavandi, 61, sought out a third party to assault his ex-wife's nephew "so he cannot talk anymore".

items.title: People with intellectual disability continue to receive substandard healthcare as program to train doctors risks closure
items.summary: Sam Stubbs’s parents were asked about his quality of life while treating him for a lung infection. They realised it was because he was born with Down syndrome.

items.title: Sydney's 2SER community radio to avoid closure under reduced model

items.title: Australian diver Josh Richards leaves Laos cave rescue
items.summary: Josh Richards is on his way back to Australia, after multiple days helping rescue miners trapped in a Laos cave.

items.title: Neo-Nazi leader Thomas Sewell covertly broadcasting hate on Instagram from Melbourne acreage
items.summary: ABC NEWS Verify can reveal that Neo-Nazi leader Thomas Sewell has been uploading antisemitic and homophobic diatribes to Instagram from a property on the outskirts of Melbourne.

items.title: Man fined for booing during Anzac Day dawn service at Sydney's Martin Place
items.summary: Eli Toby told the court that his actions were intended as a way to "stand for what I believed was the right thing at the time", but refused to apologise to the judge.

items.title: Fears of job losses as NBN confirms overhaul of television bulletins
items.summary: Media academics fear for the future of regional journalism after the drastic downsizing of one of Australia's oldest nightly news bulletins.

items.title: Teen left with fractured skull after e-scooter crash, raising 'genuine concern' over e-devices

items.title: Queensland Reds coach Les Kiss confident ahead of Super Rugby Pacific finals
items.summary: Queensland Reds coach Les Kiss knows his side will be underdogs in this weekend's Super Rugby qualifying final, a match that could be his last with the club.

items.title: No charges for Mark Bird, who Tasmanian coroner found faked wife Helen's suicide
items.summary: A man who a coroner found made his wife's 2010 death look like a suicide will not face prosecution after police said there was "insufficient evidence" to lay charges.

items.title: Influential Aboriginal leader Lowitja O'Donoghue immortalised in bronze statue
items.summary: A bronze statue of influential Aboriginal leader Lowitja O'Donoghue has been unveiled in Adelaide — the first of six monuments of prominent Indigenous South Australians that the state government has planned.

items.title: What the looming sale of Melbourne Renegades means for fans and the BBL
items.summary: If the Renegades are sold to a private buyer as expected, what could it mean for Melbourne fans if the BBL team no longer looks like itself?
